mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year, creating negative real rates that incentivize equity allocation and reduce discount rates on future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-pandemic monetary accommodation with emerging inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both trigger conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window during this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) exceeds 500k persons, signaling robust labor market recovery that increases consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2021 PAYEMS at 143048k, post-pandemic recovery phase
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS monthly change exceeds 500k, or mean forward return in triggered months is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline more than 10% month-over-month while retail sales (RSAFS) remain flat or decline, signaling weakening aggregate demand that reduces earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST MoM change < -10% AND RSAFS MoM change < 1%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when both trigger conditions are met, or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns with Rank IC > -0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises more than 0.2 percentage points, indicating supply-side contraction and labor market deterioration that compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM change < 0% AND UNRATE change > 0.2pp
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both trigger conditions are met, or Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates quarter-over-quarter while retail sales (RSAFS) grow month-over-month above 2%, indicating robust demand-side expansion that raises corporate revenue and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 52% of periods when both series show positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 6.5% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines month-over-month, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances when both trigger conditions are met, or mean forward return in triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains below 0.5% while core inflation (proxied by CPIAUCSL) remains below 2% year-over-year, indicating accommodative monetary policy with subdued inflation expectations that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) grows month-over-month above 1% while capacity utilization remains elevated, indicating strong supply-side momentum that supports corporate profitability and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:February 2021 INDPRO at 107.1843, post-pandemic production recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of months when INDPRO MoM growth exceeds 1%, or mean forward return in triggered months is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).
